creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents claiming to search the web often fail silently by hitting CAPTCHAs, anti-bot systems, or falling back to stale training data while returning answers as if successful. A demonstration using Bright Data's Web MCP shows how real web access versus blocked access produces different results, revealing reliability issues in citations, real-time data, and hallucination rates.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA demonstrated video diffusion generation at near real-time speeds on Blackwell B200 by combining quantization, caching, and step distillation to reduce diffusion steps from fifty to as few as one or four. The approach includes dynamic quantization with Black Forest Labs on Flux 2, a caching method that avoids recomputing unchanged latent chunks, and distillation methods using trajectory-based or distribution-based training. NVIDIA's open-source FastGen repository packages the post-training and GPU sharding work to apply these optimizations at scale.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: MCP and ChatGPT apps use nested iframes to isolate third-party code while maintaining security. An outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner iframe, preventing CSP violations, localStorage leaks, and sandbox escapes. Each external domain must be declared in app metadata to pass submission review.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask natural language questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion to get answers or reusable widgets without engineering requests. The LLM generates declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Three reliability features enable safe adoption: preflight sequencing that injects schema context only when invoking tools, a layering rule that directs the model to trust primary sources over its training knowledge, and query validation that tests generated SQL before hardcoding it into widgets.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that enables orchestration of multiple coding models (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers in a single system. It includes features for multi-model task routing, human-in-the-loop governance, and cross-device session persistence.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the US government's reported order to Anthropic to shut down Claude Fable 5 three days after launch, citing export-control directives and jailbreak vulnerability. He covers the reasoning behind the removal and reflects on benchmarking the model before it was pulled, comparing its performance and cost to Claude Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google released an open-source Agents CLI that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. The tool provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails, integrating with AI coding assistants like Claude and allowing deployment in minutes.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream on AI topics including coding, agents, and Claude-related tools. The session will combine discussion with chess gameplay as a casual community hangout.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive agents and self-improving company systems using autonomous AI loops. Covers practical applications in ads optimization and SEO through memory-enabled tools and frameworks.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and related capabilities for autonomous coding workflows. The video covers how /goal works, setup steps, prompting techniques, and longer-horizon task execution via /Mission exploration.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding framework or paradigm that enables developers to structure codebase workflows with auto-testing and harness setup capabilities. The tool includes workflow configuration through a WORKFLOW.md file and integration with related services like Crewlet and Superdesign.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: A video overview of AI agent frameworks and capabilities including autoresearch features, Claude Code's memory architecture, and self-evolving mechanisms in agent systems like Hermes and Openclaw.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series (Nano, Super, Ultra) with a distinctive architecture combining hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE components. The video examines how these architectural elements differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention. The model optimizes inference through tiled KV cache access patterns and I/O improvements, reducing latency in both prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code reviews Pi, an agent framework described as unique for its harness layer design. The video compares Pi against frontier agent products like Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Antigravity, examining how different products handle increasingly complex harness layers for agent capabilities.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of a Tau Scaling Law as part of ongoing semiconductor competition between China and the USA. The video examines how EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding are key areas of focus as the industry advances.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for AWS services. It improves success rates and reduces wasted effort by supplementing agents' general knowledge with current information and guardrails.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers published a walkthrough on running Claude Code agentic tool via Amazon Bedrock with data residency within AWS infrastructure. The video covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking per team, and Bedrock Guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategies in 2026 as AI automates resume screening, emphasizing networking and personal branding over mass applications. The video argues junior developers remain valuable, using a transportation analogy to suggest jobs evolve rather than disappear, and recommends building projects, blogging, contributing to open source, and developing a strong online presence.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production deployment best practices for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with OAuth and IAM isolation, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and comprehensive observability via metrics and distributed tracing. The patterns apply across infrastructure platforms and are demonstrated with the Strands Agents SDK on AWS services.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video exploring whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" practices, comparing approaches from OpenAI Codex and Claude. The content examines code generation trends at Google across 2024-2026 and references competitive tooling in the industry.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or project presented as disruptive to open source software. The video explores how Malus operates and debates whether it represents a threat or positive change for open source maintainers.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan, Stanford roboticist and founder of ART Lab, argues that the robotics industry's focus on humanoid forms may be myopic. She contends that designing robots for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, should shape the billions of robots entering our lives.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into a unified interface for enterprise decision-making. Founded by Eléonore Crespo, the company has raised over $400 million and counts Figma, Brex, and Anthropic among its customers, growing 2x year over year.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of AI-native IT automation platform Serval, argues that passionate customer enthusiasm does not prove product-market fit and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He recommends embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and advocates betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird of Koah Labs raises $26M for an ad-based monetization platform targeting AI tools, arguing that subscription models have failed AI apps and that human trust, not technology, is the primary bottleneck for AI agent adoption. He applies decision-making frameworks from competitive sailing, treating startup building as a high-variability, low-risk proposition.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
